llm_recommendations.reason_for_recommendation: Royal Enfield Scram 440, a key motorcycle model for the brand (owned by Eicher Motors), is highlighted for its versatile features, approachable ergonomics, rugged daily-rider capability, comfortable suspension, lower seat height, and competitive pricing (₹223,131 to ₹230,641 ex-showroom), enhancing its appeal.

Initial Public Offering (IPO) subscription period ended, oversubscribed 1.61 times overall. Allotment tentatively scheduled for March 20, with listing on BSE and NSE on March 24. Includes fresh issue of 75 lakh shares (Rs 240 crore) and offer-for-sale of 50 lakh shares (Rs 160 crore).

Global brokerage CLSA hiked its target price from Rs 118 to Rs 180, citing the firm's growing optical business in the US (North America revenue growth at 36% for 9M FY26, up from 25% in FY25) and improvements in its orderbook.

Shares fell sharply (5%) due to the sudden resignation of part-time chairman Atanu Chakraborty, who cited 'certain happenings and practices within the bank' over the past two years that were 'not in congruence with my personal values and ethics', raising governance concerns. Keki Mistry appointed interim chairman.
Shares fell sharply (2.56%) as rising fuel costs (Brent crude soaring past $111 per barrel due to Middle East tensions) threaten to squeeze margins for the oil-sensitive aviation sector.
Among the few stocks trading in positive territory, benefiting from the rise in energy prices amidst global tensions (Brent crude soaring past $111 per barrel).
